DANIEL REYES
Hoboken, NJ  |  daniel.reyes@email.com  |  (201) 555-0142
linkedin.com/in/danielreyes-dev  |  github.com/dreyes-eng


SUMMARY
Senior backend engineer with 7+ years building secure, distributed systems in regulated
industries. Strong background in cloud architecture, security engineering, and mentoring.


PROFESSIONAL EXPERIENCE

Senior Software Engineer
Aegis Financial Services — Jersey City, NJ
April 2022 – Present

- Led the architecture and rollout of a microservices platform processing 2.4M
  transactions per day, replacing a legacy monolith over a 14-month migration
- Designed authentication and authorization layer using OAuth 2.0, OpenID Connect,
  and JWT, reducing unauthorized access incidents by 78% year-over-year
- Architected a Kafka-based event pipeline for fraud detection, cutting end-to-end
  detection latency from 4 minutes to under 12 seconds
- Established the team's code review standards and security review checklist;
  mentored 6 engineers, 2 of whom were promoted to senior within 18 months
- Owned the AWS infrastructure for the payments service: EC2, Lambda, S3, RDS
  PostgreSQL, and CloudWatch monitoring across 3 regions
- Implemented Terraform-based infrastructure-as-code, replacing 200+ pages of
  manual runbook procedures
- Drove the adoption of OWASP secure coding practices across 4 engineering teams
- Coordinated with the compliance team for SOC 2 Type II audit, owned the
  technical evidence collection across 12 controls

Software Engineer II
Northbridge Logistics — Newark, NJ
February 2019 – March 2022 (3 years, 1 month)

- Built the warehouse management API in Java and Spring Boot, serving 47
  distribution centers and 2,800 concurrent users
- Designed the PostgreSQL schema for inventory tracking, optimizing query
  performance by 64% through indexing strategy and partitioning
- Developed REST APIs and gRPC services for the inventory and shipment domains
- Containerized 14 legacy services using Docker and orchestrated deployment with
  Kubernetes on AWS EKS
- Set up CI/CD pipelines in Jenkins and later migrated the team to GitHub Actions
- Implemented Prometheus and Grafana monitoring, established SLO definitions for
  3 critical services
- Mentored 3 junior engineers through their first production deployments
- Participated in on-call rotation; reduced mean-time-to-resolution by 41% through
  improved runbooks and observability tooling

Junior Developer
Northbridge Logistics — Newark, NJ
July 2017 – January 2019

- Wrote Java services for the order processing system
- Contributed to bug fixes, code reviews, and unit testing using JUnit
- Supported the migration from SVN to Git
- Worked on internal tools using Python and Bash scripting


EDUCATION

Master of Science in Cybersecurity
New Jersey Institute of Technology (NJIT) — Newark, NJ
September 2020 – May 2022
- Concentration: Secure Software Engineering
- Thesis: "Detecting Anomalous API Access Patterns Using Sequence Models"
- GPA: 3.87

Bachelor of Arts in Computer Science
Rutgers University–New Brunswick — New Brunswick, NJ
September 2013 – May 2017
- Minor in Mathematics
- Dean's List: Fall 2015, Spring 2016, Fall 2016
- GPA: 3.6

TECHNICAL SKILLS

Languages: Java, Python, SQL, Bash, JavaScript, Go (learning)
Backend: Spring Boot, REST APIs, gRPC, OAuth 2.0, JWT, OpenID Connect
Cloud & Infra: AWS (EC2, Lambda, S3, RDS, EKS, CloudWatch), Terraform, Docker, Kubernetes
Data: PostgreSQL, Kafka, Redis, Elasticsearch
DevOps: GitHub Actions, Jenkins, Prometheus, Grafana, Datadog
Security: OWASP, IAM, SSL/TLS, Penetration Testing concepts, SOC 2 compliance

PROFESSIONAL DEVELOPMENT

- Speaker, "Pragmatic Microservices Migration" — NJ Tech Meetup, October 2024
